channel.title: Posts on NAS | Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on NAS | Blog

item.title: 🚀 How to Set Up a Simple GitHub Actions Workflow for Hugo 🌍
item.description: Learn how to set up a simple GitHub Actions workflow to automate the deployment of your Hugo site to GitHub Pages!

item.title: Git Cheat Sheet: Beginners Guide
item.description:
Git Cheat Sheet for Beginners 🚀

Welcome to the Git Cheat Sheet, your ultimate guide to version control that will make you feel like a Git wizard (without the beard and pointy hat). If you’re new to Git, no worries! I will break things down and help you understand why and when to use each command in a fun and easy way.

Table of Contents

  • 1. Create a New Repo 🛠️
  • 2. Clone a Repo 🏡
  • 3. Check the Status 🔍
  • 4. Stage Your Changes 📋
  • 5. Commit Your Work 💾
  • 6. Pull and Push 🔄
  • 7. Branching Out 🌿
  • 8. Merging 🔥
  • 9. Viewing the History 📚
  • 10. Undo Mistakes 🔄
  • 11. Stashing Your Work 🧳
  • 12. Tags for Releases 🏷️

1. Create a New Repo 🛠️

When you start a brand new project, you need to turn your folder into a Git repository.

item.title: Building a Prop Firm Risk Calculator: A Journey in Smart Money Management
item.description:
It started with a simple observation: most prop traders, including myself, struggle with position sizing. We either risk too much during drawdowns or too little during winning streaks. After blowing a few accounts (yes, I’m looking at you, my overly aggressive 2023 self), I knew there had to be a better way.

The Eureka Moment

While studying various trading books, I stumbled upon the Kelly Criterion - a mathematical formula that professional gamblers and investors use to optimize their bet sizing. But there was a catch: the traditional Kelly formula requires you to know your win rate and risk-reward ratio beforehand. In prop trading, these numbers are constantly changing.

item.title: Ideas from Zero to One | Peter Thiel
item.description:
Introduction

When you get inspired by Bill Gates and build another five companies like Microsoft then you go from 1 to n. But when you create something unique that did not exist before, you are going from zero to one. This book is all about inspiring you to think about grand ideas and not for those who are looking to merely tinker. Unsolved problems are hotbeds of opportunity. Conventional education leads to conventional choices. So his Thiel Fellowship program funds smart people who are under 20, to forgo their college education and start their own companies.

item.title: Guide on how to make a personal website using Hugo
